meaning: adj.熟的,成熟的,时机成熟的v.成熟
examples: Reforms were promised when the time was ripe.
You'll need four ripe tomatoes, skinned and chopped.
Still, stock loan is a business that is ripe for abuse, at least according to investigators.
Richard Bove, an analyst at Ladenburg Thalmann, says this disconnect makes Lehman ripe for a hostile takeover.
